You are working on the wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 24.12 (commit: eeec5405).

## Issue to Resolve

[Reader IA] Bring back the Discover Onboarding for new users
When a new user entered the Reader for the first time we would show the `Discover` feed with a "Welcome" message and a button leading to a "Choose your topics" screen, which essentially followed those tags and made Discover be populated with stuff based on those tags.

All the code for that is still present in the app and is still shown if the user unsubscribes from ALL their tags (when the user has 0 subscribed tags), here's an example:

https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/assets/5091503/7b77b04a-fa3d-4e6a-852c-95ac587b97cd

This (0 tags subscribed) used to be the state of a new account as well, but recently the `dailyprompt` tag is being subscribed to by default on new accounts, which causes the Welcome screen to be skipped and the Reader Discover feed to be populated, which makes it hard for the user to configure their tags and see interesting stuff in that feed for the first time accessing the Reader in the app.

We want to bring back this simple Onboarding Flow to provide a better user experience, especially on the first time using the Reader.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/reader/discover/ReaderDiscoverViewModel.kt`
- `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/reader/discover/interests/ReaderInterestsViewModel.kt`
- `WordPress/src/main/res/layout/reader_interests_fragment_layout.xml`
- `WordPress/src/main/res/values/strings.xml`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

content.thinking:
**Implementing onboarding flow**

I need to create an onboarding flow for new users, particularly concerning the default subscribed tag, dailyprompt. The existing welcome screen only appears if zero tags are subscribed. So, I think we should treat the "default tag" as not counting for onboarding purposes. I’ll need to look closely at the code and modify the ReaderDiscoverViewModel and likely the ReaderInterestsViewModel to check for user-configurable tag counts, excluding the default. I'll also ensure the welcome message doesn't show if the user has previously visited Reader.**Detecting first-time users**

I’m thinking I need to ensure that the welcome message displays for first-time users in Reader, even if they have the dailyprompt tag. To detect if it's their first time, I might use the AppPrefs value for hasSeenReaderDiscoverOnboarding. I should search the codebase to confirm how this is implemented. Let’s use grep to look for instances of “dailyprompt” and “welcome” to help clarify how the logic works. That way, I can figure out the necessary adjustments.

content.text:
Implemented “Discover onboarding” restore by treating the default `dailyprompt` tag as *not counting* toward “user has followed topics”:

- **`ReaderDiscoverViewModel.kt`**: when the followed tag list is empty *or contains only* `dailyprompt` (and `dailyprompt-*`), the Discover screen now shows the existing Welcome / “Get Started” empty state that opens topic selection.
- **`ReaderInterestsViewModel.kt`**: when opened from Discover, the Interests screen no longer immediately closes if the only followed tag is `dailyprompt`; it will load the “Choose your topics” flow as intended.

No UI/string changes were required since the onboarding UI already existed and was being shown for the “0 tags” case.

125→    private fun ReaderDiscoverFragmentLayoutBinding.initViewModel() {
126→        viewModel = ViewModelProvider(this@ReaderDiscoverFragment, viewModelFactory)
127→            .get(ReaderDiscoverViewModel::class.java)
128→        parentViewModel = ViewModelProvider(requireParentFragment()).get(ReaderViewModel::class.java)
129→
130→        viewModel.uiState.observe(viewLifecycleOwner) {
131→            when (it) {
132→                is DiscoverUiState.ContentUiState -> {
133→                    (recyclerView.adapter as ReaderDiscoverAdapter).update(it.cards)
134→                }
135→                is DiscoverUiState.EmptyUiState -> {
136→                    uiHelpers.setTextOrHide(actionableEmptyView.title, it.titleResId)
137→                    uiHelpers.setTextOrHide(actionableEmptyView.subtitle, it.subTitleRes)
138→                    uiHelpers.setImageOrHide(actionableEmptyView.image, it.illustrationResId)
139→                    uiHelpers.setTextOrHide(actionableEmptyView.button, it.buttonResId)
140→                    actionableEmptyView.button.setOnClickListener { _ -> it.action.invoke() }
141→                }
142→                is DiscoverUiState.LoadingUiState -> Unit // Do nothing
143→            }
144→
145→            uiHelpers.updateVisibility(recyclerView, it.contentVisiblity)
146→            uiHelpers.updateVisibility(progressBar, it.fullscreenProgressVisibility)
147→            uiHelpers.updateVisibility(progressText, it.fullscreenProgressVisibility)
148→            uiHelpers.updateVisibility(progressLoadingMore, it.loadMoreProgressVisibility)
149→            uiHelpers.updateVisibility(actionableEmptyView, it.fullscreenEmptyVisibility)
150→            ptrLayout.isEnabled = it.swipeToRefreshEnabled
151→            ptrLayout.isRefreshing = it.reloadProgressVisibility
152→        }
153→        viewModel.scrollToTopEvent.observeEvent(viewLifecycleOwner) { recyclerView.scrollToPosition(0) }
154→        viewModel.navigationEvents.observeEvent(viewLifecycleOwner) { handleNavigation(it) }
155→        viewModel.snackbarEvents.observeEvent(viewLifecycleOwner) { it.showSnackbar() }
156→        viewModel.preloadPostEvents.observeEvent(viewLifecycleOwner) { it.addWebViewCachingFragment() }
157→        viewModel.start(parentViewModel)
158→    }

160→    @Suppress("ComplexMethod")
161→    private fun handleNavigation(event: ReaderNavigationEvents) = when (event) {
162→        is ShowPostDetail -> ReaderActivityLauncher.showReaderPostDetail(context, event.post.blogId, event.post.postId)
163→        is SharePost -> ReaderActivityLauncher.sharePost(context, event.post)
164→        is OpenPost -> ReaderActivityLauncher.openPost(context, event.post)
165→        is ShowReaderComments -> ReaderActivityLauncher.showReaderComments(
166→            context,
167→            event.blogId,
168→            event.postId,
169→            READER_POST_CARD.sourceDescription
170→        )
171→        is ShowNoSitesToReblog -> ReaderActivityLauncher.showNoSiteToReblog(activity)
172→        is ShowSitePickerForResult -> ActivityLauncher.showSitePickerForResult(
173→            this@ReaderDiscoverFragment,
174→            event.preselectedSite,
175→            event.mode
176→        )
177→        is OpenEditorForReblog -> ActivityLauncher.openEditorForReblog(activity, event.site, event.post, event.source)
178→        is ShowBookmarkedTab -> ActivityLauncher.viewSavedPostsListInReader(activity)
179→        is ShowBookmarkedSavedOnlyLocallyDialog -> showBookmarkSavedLocallyDialog(event)
180→        is ShowPostsByTag -> ReaderActivityLauncher.showReaderTagPreview(
181→            context,
182→            event.tag,
183→            ReaderTracker.SOURCE_DISCOVER,
184→            readerTracker
185→        )
186→        is ShowVideoViewer -> ReaderActivityLauncher.showReaderVideoViewer(context, event.videoUrl)
187→        is ShowBlogPreview -> ReaderActivityLauncher.showReaderBlogOrFeedPreview(
188→            context,
189→            event.siteId,
190→            event.feedId,
191→            event.isFollowed,
192→            ReaderTracker.SOURCE_DISCOVER,
193→            readerTracker
194→        )
195→        is ShowReportPost -> ReaderActivityLauncher.openUrl(
196→            context,
197→            readerUtilsWrapper.getReportPostUrl(event.url),
198→            OpenUrlType.INTERNAL
199→        )
200→        is ShowReportUser -> ReaderActivityLauncher.openUrl(
201→            context,
202→            readerUtilsWrapper.getReportUserUrl(event.url, event.authorId),
203→            OpenUrlType.INTERNAL
204→        )
205→        is ShowReaderSubs -> ReaderActivityLauncher.showReaderSubs(context)
206→        else -> Unit // Do Nothing
207→    }

209→    private fun showBookmarkSavedLocallyDialog(bookmarkDialog: ShowBookmarkedSavedOnlyLocallyDialog) {
210→        if (bookmarksSavedLocallyDialog == null) {
211→            MaterialAlertDialogBuilder(requireActivity())
212→                .setTitle(getString(bookmarkDialog.title))
213→                .setMessage(getString(bookmarkDialog.message))
214→                .setPositiveButton(getString(bookmarkDialog.buttonLabel)) { _, _ ->
215→                    bookmarkDialog.okButtonAction.invoke()
216→                }
217→                .setOnDismissListener {
218→                    bookmarksSavedLocallyDialog = null
219→                }
220→                .setCancelable(false)
221→                .create()
222→                .let {
223→                    bookmarksSavedLocallyDialog = it
224→                    it.show()
225→                }
226→        }
227→    }

240→            }
